name: French Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Measure warm water in a mixing bowl.
2. Add yeast, stirring with a fork until dissolved.
3. Stir in the salt and 3 cups of the flour.
4. Beat vigorously with a rubber spatula until the batter is smooth.
5. Gradually add enough flour to make a soft, workable dough.
6. Remove dough to a floured board. Knead, adding more flour if necessary, for 10-12 minutes or until smooth and elastic with bubbles underneath the skin.
7. Place dough in a large, warm, greased bowl, turning to coat the top. Use a bowl with vertical sides rather than one that flares out.
8. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and a towel. Set bowl in a protected spot and let dough triple in bulk, about 2 1/2 hours.
9. Punch down the dough, knead lightly in the bowl, re-cover, and allow to double, about 45 minutes to an hour.
10. Turn out on a floured surface, knead lightly, and divide into three portions. Cover and let rest for 15 minutes.
11. Grease baking sheets and sprinkle lightly with cornmeal. Heavy French baking sheets do not need greasing.
Three methods of forming French loaves:
1. Press one portion of dough into an oval. If the dough pulls back, cover and let rest a few more minutes. Make an indentation down the center of the oval using the side of your hand. Fold dough over and pinch sides together. The dough should now begin to have an elongated shape. Roll back and forth to a length that will fit your baking sheet. Place on prepared pan, seam side down. Tuck in sides and ends with fingers to smooth out the long, slender shape. Repeat with remaining dough. Cover and allow to double in bulk, about 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
2. Roll a portion of dough into a rectangle, approximately 9x16 inches. Roll tightly from the long side, pinching sides and ends to seal. Roll back and forth, smoothing outwards into desired length. Place on prepared baking pan, seam side down, tucking in sides and ends with fingertips. Cover and let rise for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
3. Round loaf: With your hands, begin rounding a portion of dough into a ball. Smooth the dough by pulling tightly with one hand across the top and underneath the ball, using the other hand to keep turning until you have a round, satiny loaf. Scoop up with a dough scraper and place on prepared baking sheet. Cover and let rise until doubled, about an hour.
Two methods of Baking:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit. You will need: a razor blade, cold water, soft pastry brush, and an atomizer containing water.
- Hold the razor at a flat angle and make 3 long, diagonal slashes, approximately 1/4 to 1/2 inch deep in each loaf.
- Make a cross or two straight slashes on round loaves.
- Brush with cold water. Place loaves on the middle shelf in the hot oven and quickly spray with water using an atomizer to create steam. Set timer for 5 minutes, then spray again. Repeat spraying at 5-minute intervals for a total of 2 more times. Bake for an additional 10 minutes. Cool on wire racks.
2. Preheat oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit. You will need: a razor blade and egg wash (beat 1 egg with 1 tablespoon water until frothy).
- Slash loaves as directed in Method 1.
- Bake loaves on the middle shelf for 15 minutes. Remove and brush thoroughly with the egg wash. Return to oven and bake an additional 10 minutes. Cool on wire racks.
French bread freezes exceptionally well. This works particularly well if you have baking tiles. If using, heat them at 500 degrees Fahrenheit for an hour, watching the bread carefully because baking time will be reduced. Reduce oven temperature before putting the bread in!
